title: Operators and Control Flow
learningObjectives: Use arithmetic, relational, and logical operators correctly
Write clean if/else and switch logic
Apply break and continue in controlled way
Avoid infinite loops and branch bugs
Dry-run code execution for interview questions

sections.title: Why Operators and Control Flow matters
sections.content:
Control flow is how your program decides and repeats work. Even if syntax is right, incorrect conditions can break business logic.

Interviewers use control-flow problems to test logical thinking. They watch how you handle branches, boundaries, and loop exits.

In real systems, a wrong condition can trigger billing issues, repeated API calls, or missing validation checks. Learning this early prevents expensive bugs later.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Operators and Control Flow
sections.content:
### Operators
- Arithmetic: +, -, *, /, %
- Relational: ==, !=, >, <, >=, <=
- Logical: &&, ||, !
- Ternary: condition ? A : B

### Control statements
- if / else if / else
- switch
- for, while, do-while
- break, continue

### Key ideas
- Use parentheses to avoid precedence confusion.
- Keep branch conditions readable.
- Always test loop start, condition, and increment.

### Common logic bugs
- Off-by-one loop bounds
- Missing default case in switch
- Using assignment (=) by mistake where comparison (==) intended
- Complex nested conditions without helper variables

### Interview tip
Before coding, state condition logic in plain English. Then translate to Java.

### Satyverse — deeper path (theory → practice)
**Control flow is about expressing intent.** Prefer `switch` (Java 17+ patterns) when mapping discrete values; use `if` for ranges and compound business rules so the next reader sees the *why*.

**Loops:** `for-each` when you only read; indexed `for` when you need the index or mutate positions; `while` when the exit condition is not a simple counter (streams, iterators).

**Ternary vs if:** ternaries for short assignments; never nest three levels — that is a maintenance trap.

**Debugging tip:** when a condition “should” fire but does not, print the *types* and values of operands (`==` on `Integer` vs `int` is a classic silent bug).

sections.title: Basic Walkthrough
sections.description: if / else-if ladder: classify a numeric score into bands (same pattern as many business rules).

sections.title: Intermediate Implementation
sections.output:
Odd number: 1
Odd number: 3
Odd number: 5
Odd number: 7
sections.description: for-loop with continue (skip evens) and break (stop early) — control flow in one place.

sections.title: Advanced Production Pattern
sections.description: Compound boolean guards: age + ID checks with early returns (readable validation style).

sections.title: Architecture View - Day 3
sections.description: Visual model for Operators and Control Flow

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Writing unreadable nested if blocks without helper variables.
Forgetting break in old-style switch cases.
Using continue or break without understanding effect.
Not testing negative and boundary inputs.

sections.title: Exercise - Implement Operators and Control Flow
sections.problem:
Implement a Java program for **Operators and Control Flow** with real input and edge-case handling.

Requirements:
1. Accept at least 5 sample cases.
2. Print clear intermediate output.
3. Include one invalid/boundary case.
4. Explain time complexity in comments.
5. Keep naming and structure interview-ready.
hints: Start with a straightforward solution first.
Write at least one edge case test.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is short-circuit evaluation in && and ||?
conceptual.answer: For &&, if the left operand is false, the right is not evaluated. For ||, if the left is true, the right is skipped.

followUps.question: Interview follow-up: how does **Operators and Control Flow** show up in **production** debugging or **on-call**?
followUps.answer: Tie **logs**, **metrics**, **repro** steps, and **ownership** to what you already said. **Interviewers** reward **symptom → cause → fix → verification**, not textbook **definitions** alone. For &&, if the left operand is false, the right is not evaluated. For ||, if the left is true, the right is skipped. Connect the idea to something you can **observe** in **logs**, **tests**, or a **local run**.

conceptual.question: Difference between while and do-while?
conceptual.answer: while checks condition before each iteration; do-while checks after, so the body runs at least once.

conceptual.question: What is the difference between break and continue?
conceptual.answer: break exits the innermost loop or switch; continue skips to the next iteration of the innermost loop.

conceptual.question: What is wrong with if (x = 5) in Java?
conceptual.answer: Single = is assignment, not comparison; this is a compile error for boolean context unless x is boolean (rare pitfall).

conceptual.answer: It is an expression that returns a value and must be used in a value context; both branches should be type-compatible.

conceptual.answer: They jump out of nested loops but can harm readability; refactoring to methods is often clearer.

conceptual.question: How do you avoid off-by-one errors in for loops?
conceptual.answer: Clearly define inclusive/exclusive end, use i < length vs i <= length-1 consistently, and test with 0, 1, and max-size inputs.

conceptual.question: What is the difference between | and || for booleans?
conceptual.answer: | always evaluates both sides; || short-circuits. For booleans, || is usually preferred for safety and performance.

codeBased.question: Write a for-loop that prints only odd numbers from 1 to 20.
codeBased.answer: for (int i=1;i<=20;i+=2) System.out.println(i); or use i%2!=0 inside loop.

seniorScenario.question: A pricing rule engine has nested if-else bugs in production. Approach?
seniorScenario.answer: Extract rules into table-driven or strategy classes, add unit tests per rule branch, and use coverage tools.

followUps.question: How do you **communicate** **impact**, **ETA**, and **unknowns** in this **Operators and Control Flow** situation?

Context: Extract rules into table-driven or strategy classes, add unit tests per rule branch, and use coverage tools. Tie to **error budget** or **team** policy when relevant. Extract rules into table-driven or strategy classes, add unit tests per rule branch, and use coverage tools. seniorScenario.question: A switch on enum missed a new enum constant after upgrade—runtime issue?
seniorScenario.answer: With switch expressions on enums, exhaustiveness can be compile-time checked; add default or handle new cases when enum grows.

wrongAnswers: switch without break is always safe in Java.
The condition in while(true) can never cause an infinite loop.
x = 5 and x == 5 mean the same in an if condition.
|| and | behave identically for all boolean expressions.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Area | Rule | Interview Tip | Gotcha |
|---|---|---|---|
| Definition | Give 1-line meaning first | Keep answer structured | Avoid vague statements |
| Syntax | Show minimal working code | Explain output | Do not skip imports/signature |
| Edge Cases | Always test boundaries | Mention at least one | Most bugs live here |
| Debugging | Print intermediate states | Explain failure reason | Guessing wastes time |
| Quality | Prefer clear names | Talk about readability | Clever but unclear code fails review |
